content:
Hello there, My name is Bakti Satria Adhityatama.
Welcome to Blog & Portfolio Website. here I Write anything about Cyber Security, Data Science, and Artificial Intelligence related things.
I’m Currently a candidate of full-ride scholarship from Telkom (GPSP - Great People Scholarship Awards), thus currently applying for MBA and Msc in Top 10 Universities on US
I’m a full-time employee of Telkom Indonesia as a data Scientist
I’m a husband, and father of one adorable daughter
I’m currently learning data structures and algorithms while attempting to solve leetcode problems
I have BSc and MSc. in Electrical Engineering From Institut Teknologi Bandung (ITB)
I have a big interest in Cyber Security & Data Science field
Professional Works and Experiences 2024-now - Cyber Security Solution Engineer at Telkom Indonesia
2023-now - Data Scientist at Telkom Indonesia’ Department of Digital Business
2020-2022 - Asset Insurance & Physical Security Engineer at Telkom Indonesia’s Asset Management Center Unit
2017-2019 - IT & Asset Database officer at Telkom Indonesia’s Asset Management Center Unit
2016 - Backend Developer at Meridian.id a local software house company in Bandung
Research Interest Machine Learning and AI: In late 2022, I worked part-time as a research assistant at Institut Teknologi Bandung. I participated in research on wearable device technology for vital sign monitoring using mobile phones for non-contact photoplethysmography (PPG) measurements. My role was primarily focused on deploying the mobile application and the API for the Deep Learning Model.
Social Network Analysis: In my Master’s Degree Thesis, I evaluated the performance of various algorithms for graph sampling in social media networks. I proposed enhanced methods suitable for networks with specific properties. The results of the graph sampling were then used for in-depth Social Network Analysis (SNA) on Twitter.
Internet of Things & Embedded Systems: For my Bachelor’s Degree Thesis, I developed a fully functional prototype of an Electronic Road Pricing (ERP) System. I also designed a web application to monitor the ERP prototype and was responsible for ensuring the seamless API communication between the server and the mobile application.
Cyber Security and Privacy - This field is more than just an area of study for me; it’s a passion.
Why do I make this blog ? In my free time, I engage in various activities, such as reading books, taking online courses, solving leetcode & kaggle problems, and exploring labs in hacking playgrounds like Hackthebox and Tryhackme. Given my hobbies and my role in the tech industry, I began blogging to validate and solidify my understanding of specific topics. As the saying goes If you can’t explain it simply, you don’t understand it well enough.
Currently, I maintain this blog to document everything I learn on my journey to achieving the OSCP certification. The primary resource for my learning is this list of OSCP-Like machines on Hackthebox from TjNull. I also use this platform to chronicle my side projects and endeavors in the Data Science & AI domains.
I relish challenges and continuously push myself towards achieving greater goals. Most of the knowledge I possess comes from self-teaching and exploration. I genuinely believe that writing is one of the most potent tools to reinforce the information in my mind. Moreover, it’s my guiding principle to remain a lifelong learner and always seek profound knowledge.
I also curate this blog to express heartfelt gratitude to everyone who has indirectly aided me with their writings, guides, blogs, etc., especially when I encounter challenges. I hope this chain of knowledge continues unbroken. I also want to make sure that I ‘OWN’ my blog, by writing contents using Static-Site-Generator SSG like Hugo like this, and hosted in Github.
Additionally, I maintain another non-technical blog on WordPress (written in Bahasa Indonesia) that captures my daily reflections. You can find it here.

Welcome to Blog & Portfolio Website. here I Write anything about Cyber Security, Data Science, and Artificial Intelligence related things.

  • I’m Currently a candidate of full-ride scholarship from Telkom (GPSP - Great People Scholarship Awards), thus currently applying for MBA and Msc in Top 10 Universities on US

  • I’m a full-time employee of Telkom Indonesia as a data Scientist

content:
Exploring Markdown Styles in Obsidian Welcome to this exploration of all basic Markdown styles. Markdown allows you to format your notes easily in Obsidian. Let’s go through the common elements.
1. Headings Headings are used to create sections and subsections in your notes.
Heading 3 Heading 4 Heading 5 2. Bold and Italics You can emphasize text using bold or italics:
Bold: **This text is bold** Italics: *This text is italic* Combined: ***Bold and italic combined*** 3. Lists Unordered List Item 1 Item 2 Sub-item 1 Sub-item 2 Ordered List First item Second item Third item 4. Links You can include links to websites or internal notes.
External Link to Google Link to a note in Obsidian 5. Images Images can be embedded with the following syntax:
6. Code You can include both inline and block code.
Inline Code Use backticks for inline code: `inline code`
Block Code def hello_world(): print("Hello, world!")
summary:
Exploring Markdown Styles in Obsidian

Welcome to this exploration of all basic Markdown styles. Markdown allows you to format your notes easily in Obsidian. Let’s go through the common elements.

1. Headings

Headings are used to create sections and subsections in your notes.

Heading 3

Heading 4

Heading 5

2. Bold and Italics

You can emphasize text using bold or italics:

  • Bold: **This text is bold**
  • Italics: *This text is italic*
  • Combined: ***Bold and italic combined***

3. Lists

Unordered List

  • Item 1
  • Item 2
    • Sub-item 1
    • Sub-item 2

Ordered List

  1. First item
  2. Second item
  3. Third item

4. Links

You can include links to websites or internal notes.
title: First Post

content:
Exploring Markdown Styles in Obsidian Welcome to this exploration of all basic Markdown styles. Markdown allows you to format your notes easily in Obsidian. Let’s go through the common elements.
1. Headings Headings are used to create sections and subsections in your notes.
Heading 3 Heading 4 Heading 5 2. Bold and Italics You can emphasize text using bold or italics:
Bold: **This text is bold** Italics: *This text is italic* Combined: ***Bold and italic combined*** 3. Lists Unordered List Item 1 Item 2 Sub-item 1 Sub-item 2 Ordered List First item Second item Third item 4. Links You can include links to websites or internal notes.
External Link to Google Link to a note in Obsidian 5. Images Images can be embedded with the following syntax:
6. Code You can include both inline and block code.
Inline Code Use backticks for inline code: `inline code`
Block Code def hello_world(): print("Hello, world!") 7. Blockquotes Blockquotes are used to highlight important information.
This is a blockquote. You can use it for quotes or important notes.
8. Callouts Callouts help emphasize specific information. Here are some examples:
Note [!note] Important Note Remember to backup your work regularly.
Warning [!warning] Caution Make sure to check for updates before proceeding.
Tip [!tip] Helpful Tip When using Obsidian, you can link your notes using [[note name]].
Success [!success] Success! You’ve completed the Markdown tutorial.
9. Tables You can organize data in tables for easy reading.
Name Age Location Alice 30 London Bob 25 New York Charlie 35 Tokyo 10. Strikethrough You can use strikethrough to indicate deleted or incorrect text.
~~This is a strikethrough~~ will show as This is a strikethrough 11. Task Lists You can create a task list using checkboxes.
Task 1 Task 2 (completed) Task 3 Conclusion This is just a basic overview of the different Markdown styles in Obsidian. You can combine these styles to create rich, well-structured notes!
summary:

2. Bold and Italics

  • Bold: **This text is bold**
  • Italics: *This text is italic*
  • Combined: ***Bold and italic combined***

3. Lists

Unordered List

  • Item 1
  • Item 2
    • Sub-item 1
    • Sub-item 2

Ordered List

  1. First item
  2. Second item
  3. Third item

4. Links

You can include links to websites or internal notes.
